title: Quality Control Manager (3 years contract/ Research)
description:
Responsibilities

·               Develop and drive a strategic roadmap for yield improvement and defect reduction across critical process modules.

·               Lead the design and maintenance of advanced, real-time Statistical Process Control (SPC), Fault Detection and Classification (FDC), and Advanced Process Control (APC) systems to stabilize and optimize all fabrication steps.

·               Cross-contamination management to achieve balance between research and development flexibilities in integration runsheet flow path vs process tools health and process repeatability.

·               Small volume prototype protocol and product and process control plan alignment and set-up with requestor, process margin runs till demonstration of product SPC repeatability and line defect and electrical yield.

·               Serve as the technical expert and primary quality interface for key customers, leading customer audits, addressing quality-related concerns, and driving customer-specific quality improvement plans.

·               Direct and oversee high-level, cross-functional root-cause analysis (RCA) and corrective and preventive action (CAPA) teams for major process excursions, non-conformances, and field returns, utilizing methodologies like 8D or Six Sigma

·               Oversee the selection, qualification, and maintenance of all in-line and off-line metrology and defect inspection tools, ensuring measurement system accuracy (Gage R&R) and high data quality.

·               Collaborate with Process Integration and R&D teams to define, qualify, and stabilize robust manufacturing process windows for all new product introductions (NPI), ensuring Design for Manufacturability (DFM) principles are met.

·               Leverage large-scale manufacturing data (Big Data) to identify subtle process drifts, predict failures, and proactively implement controls.

·               Lead, mentor, and manage a team of Quality Engineers, Process Control Engineers, and Technicians, setting performance goals, developing career paths, and fostering a culture of ownership and data-driven decision-making.

·               Collaborate closely with all stakeholders to balance the trade-off between quality stability, process efficiency, and tool uptime.

Requirements

·               Degree in Engineering, Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, or a related technical field.

·               Preferable 10+ years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with a minimum of 5 years in a leadership/management role focusing on Process Control, Quality Assurance, or Yield Engineering within a Fab environment.

·               Deep technical knowledge of semiconductor fabrication processes in different modules.

·               Expert-level knowledge of Statistical Process Control (SPC), Design of Experiments (DOE), Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA), and Lean/Six Sigma methodologies (Black Belt certification highly preferred).

To find out more about this opportunity, please contact Lionel Liew at lionel.liew@gmprecruit.com.

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

GMP Technologies (S) Pte Ltd  |   EA Licence: 11C3793 |   EA Personnel: Lionel Liew   |   Registration No: R1330693

This is in partnership with the Employment and Employability Institute Pte Ltd (“e2i”).

e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions. e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives.

By applying for this role, you consent to GMP Technologies (S) Pte Ltd’s PDPA and e2i’s PDPA.
